assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 21-29 detail all 5 criteria: single license filter, comma-separated licenses, invalid license returns 400, pagination integration, response shape unchanged. Each criterion file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) individually shows 'Verdict: PASS' with detailed reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files exactly match the task-specified files (3 of 3)'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate' confirms no sub-tasks were created.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are report/analysis files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). The outputs contain only verification analysis and no evidence of code modifications. The report references reading code (e.g., 'The PR adds a license field...', 'The validate_license_param function...') but never describes writing, editing, or modifying code files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents findings ('All acceptance criteria are satisfied by the implementation', 'No security concerns, scope issues, or convention violations were identified'). There is no merge action, no gh pr merge command, and no mention of auto-merging. The report is purely informational.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Single new test file added (tests/api/package.rs, 80 lines, 4 test functions); no modified or deleted test files'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all expected verdicts: Scope Containment (PASS, line 7), Diff Size (PASS, line 8), Commit Traceability (N/A, line 9) from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns (PASS, line 10) from Security; CI Status (PASS, line 11), Acceptance Criteria (PASS, line 12), Verification Commands (N/A, line 15) from Correctness; Test Quality (PASS, line 13 — mentions 'no repetitive test patterns detected', 'All tests documented with doc comments', and 'Eval Quality: N/A') and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE, line 14) from Style/Conventions. Additionally Review Feedback (N/A, line 5) and Root-Cause Investigation (N/A, line 6) are present.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Beyond the summary table, report.md lines 19-33 provide detailed per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness) with specific code references (e.g., 'validate_license_param function splits on commas', 'Expression::parse rejects invalid SPDX identifiers', 'is_in with an inner join on package_license'). Criterion files 1-5 provide extensive detailed findings with code snippets and evidence sections. The summary table itself includes specific evidence: scope containment mentions '3 of 3' files, sensitive patterns mentions 'added lines', test quality mentions 'doc comments' and 'no repetitive test patterns', test change classification details '80 lines, 4 test functions'. The report has file-by-file scope comparison (line 7: 'PR files exactly match the task-specified files'), line-level pattern scanning (line 10), per-criterion verification (lines 21-29 and criterion files), and test quality assessment (line 13, line 31).

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | All tests documented with doc comments; no repetitive test patterns detected; Eval Quality: N/A'. Eval Quality is explicitly listed as N/A within the Test Quality row, confirming it does not affect the combined Test Quality verdict.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 13). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the outputs. Root-Cause Investigation (line 6) confirms 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. Both appear in the summary table as top-level rows separate from the domain sub-agent verdicts, consistent with orchestrator-level determination.
